root_text.sn55.54:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sakkesu viharati kapilavatthusmiṁ nigrodhārāme.
root_text.sn55.54:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhū bhagavato cīvarakammaṁ karonti:
root_text.sn55.54:1.7: Atha kho mahānāmo sakko yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho mahānāmo sakko bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn55.54:1.11: Na kho netaṁ, bhante, bhagavato sammukhā sutaṁ sammukhā paṭiggahitaṁ
root_text.sn55.54:1.12: sappaññena upāsakena sappañño upāsako ābādhiko dukkhito bāḷhagilāno ovaditabbo”ti.
root_text.sn55.54:2.1: “Sappaññena, mahānāma, upāsakena sappañño upāsako ābādhiko dukkhito bāḷhagilāno catūhi assāsanīyehi dhammehi assāsetabbo:
root_text.sn55.54:2.3: atthāyasmato buddhe aveccappasādo itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.54:3.1: Sappaññena, mahānāma, upāsakena sappañño upāsako ābādhiko dukkhito bāḷhagilāno imehi catūhi assāsanīyehi dhammehi assāsetvā evamassa vacanīyo:
root_text.sn55.54:3.4: ‘atthi me mātāpitūsu apekkhā’ti, so evamassa vacanīyo:
root_text.sn55.54:3.7: no cepāyasmā mātāpitūsu apekkhaṁ karissati, marissateva.
root_text.sn55.54:3.8: Sādhāyasmā, yā te mātāpitūsu apekkhā taṁ pajahā’ti.
root_text.sn55.54:4.2: ‘yā me mātāpitūsu apekkhā sā pahīnā’ti, so evamassa vacanīyo:
root_text.sn55.54:4.5: ‘atthi me puttadāresu apekkhā’ti, so evamassa vacanīyo:
root_text.sn55.54:4.8: no cepāyasmā puttadāresu apekkhaṁ karissati, marissateva.
root_text.sn55.54:4.9: Sādhāyasmā, yā te puttadāresu apekkhā taṁ pajahā’ti.
root_text.sn55.54:5.2: ‘yā me puttadāresu apekkhā sā pahīnā’ti, so evamassa vacanīyo:
root_text.sn55.54:5.5: ‘atthi me mānusakesu pañcasu kāmaguṇesu apekkhā’ti, so evamassa vacanīyo:
root_text.sn55.54:5.6: ‘mānusakehi kho, āvuso, kāmehi dibbā kāmā abhikkantatarā ca paṇītatarā ca.
root_text.sn55.54:5.7: Sādhāyasmā, mānusakehi kāmehi cittaṁ vuṭṭhāpetvā cātumahārājikesu devesu cittaṁ adhimocehī’ti.
root_text.sn55.54:6.2: ‘mānusakehi me kāmehi cittaṁ vuṭṭhitaṁ, cātumahārājikesu devesu cittaṁ adhimocitan’ti, so evamassa vacanīyo:
root_text.sn55.54:6.3: ‘cātumahārājikehi kho, āvuso, devehi tāvatiṁsā devā abhikkantatarā ca paṇītatarā ca.
root_text.sn55.54:6.4: Sādhāyasmā, cātumahārājikehi devehi cittaṁ vuṭṭhāpetvā tāvatiṁsesu devesu cittaṁ adhimocehī’ti.
root_text.sn55.54:7.2: ‘cātumahārājikehi me devehi cittaṁ vuṭṭhitaṁ, tāvatiṁsesu devesu cittaṁ adhimocitan’ti, so evamassa vacanīyo:
root_text.sn55.54:7.7: paranimmitavasavattīhi kho, āvuso, devehi brahmaloko abhikkantataro ca paṇītataro ca.
root_text.sn55.54:7.8: Sādhāyasmā, paranimmitavasavattīhi devehi cittaṁ vuṭṭhāpetvā brahmaloke cittaṁ adhimocehī’ti.
root_text.sn55.54:7.10: ‘paranimmitavasavattīhi me devehi cittaṁ vuṭṭhitaṁ, brahmaloke cittaṁ adhimocitan’ti, so evamassa vacanīyo:
root_text.sn55.54:7.12: Sādhāyasmā, brahmalokā cittaṁ vuṭṭhāpetvā sakkāyanirodhe cittaṁ upasaṁharāhī’ti.
root_text.sn55.54:8.2: ‘brahmalokā me cittaṁ vuṭṭhitaṁ, sakkāyanirodhe cittaṁ upasaṁharāmī’ti;
root_text.sn55.54:8.3: evaṁ vimuttacittassa kho, mahānāma, upāsakassa āsavā vimuttacittena bhikkhunā na kiñci nānākaraṇaṁ vadāmi, yadidaṁ—

translation_text.sn55.54:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Sakyans, near Kapilavatthu in the Banyan Tree Monastery.
translation_text.sn55.54:1.2: At that time several mendicants were making a robe for the Buddha, thinking that
translation_text.sn55.54:1.3: when his robe was finished and the three months of the rains residence had passed the Buddha would set out wandering.
translation_text.sn55.54:1.4: Mahānāma the Sakyan heard about this.
translation_text.sn55.54:1.7: Then he went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him that he had heard that the Buddha was leaving. He added,
translation_text.sn55.54:1.11: “Sir, I haven’t heard and learned it in the presence of the Buddha
translation_text.sn55.54:1.12: how a wise lay follower should advise another wise lay follower who is sick, suffering, gravely ill.”
translation_text.sn55.54:2.1: “Mahānāma, a wise lay follower should put at ease another wise lay follower who is sick, suffering, gravely ill with four consolations.
translation_text.sn55.54:2.3: You have experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.54:2.7: And you have the ethical conduct loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.’
translation_text.sn55.54:3.1: When a wise lay follower has put at ease another wise lay follower who is sick, suffering, gravely ill with these four consolations, they should say:
translation_text.sn55.54:3.2: ‘Are you concerned for your mother and father?’
translation_text.sn55.54:3.5: ‘But sir, it’s your nature to die.
translation_text.sn55.54:3.6: Whether or not you are concerned for your mother and father,
translation_text.sn55.54:3.8: It would be good to give up concern for your mother and father.’
translation_text.sn55.54:4.2: ‘I have given up concern for my mother and father,’ they should say:
translation_text.sn55.54:4.3: ‘But are you concerned for your partners and children?’
translation_text.sn55.54:4.6: ‘But sir, it’s your nature to die.
translation_text.sn55.54:4.7: Whether or not you are concerned for your partners and children,
translation_text.sn55.54:4.9: It would be good to give up concern for your partners and children.’
translation_text.sn55.54:5.2: ‘I have given up concern for my partners and children,’ they should say:
translation_text.sn55.54:5.3: ‘But are you concerned for the five kinds of human sensual stimulation?’
translation_text.sn55.54:5.6: ‘Respectable sir, heavenly sensual pleasures are better than human sensual pleasures.
translation_text.sn55.54:5.7: It would be good to turn your mind away from human sensual pleasures and focus it on the gods of the four great kings.’
translation_text.sn55.54:6.2: ‘I have done so,’ they should say:
translation_text.sn55.54:6.3: ‘Respectable sir, the gods of the thirty-three are better than the gods of the four great kings …
translation_text.sn55.54:7.3: Respectable sir, the gods of Yama …
translation_text.sn55.54:7.5: the gods who love to create …
translation_text.sn55.54:7.6: the gods who control what is created by others …
translation_text.sn55.54:7.7: the gods of the realm of divinity are better than the gods who control what is created by others.
translation_text.sn55.54:7.8: It would be good to turn your mind away from the gods who control what is created by others and focus it on the gods of the realm of divinity.’
translation_text.sn55.54:7.10: ‘I have done so,’ they should say:
translation_text.sn55.54:7.11: ‘Respectable sir, the realm of divinity is impermanent, not lasting, and included within substantial reality.
translation_text.sn55.54:7.12: It would be good to turn your mind away from the realm of divinity and apply it to the cessation of substantial reality.’
translation_text.sn55.54:8.3: then there is no difference between a lay follower whose mind is freed in this way and a mendicant whose mind is freed from defilements; that is,
translation_text.sn55.54:8.4: between the freedom of one and the other.”

variant_text.sn55.54:1.11: Na kho netaṁ → na kho te etaṁ (bj, pts1ed)
